title: 50-100x isn't the problem. Unsized 50-100x is.
_note: EVERGREEN v2 (#MindOverMarket). Retoned to Variant B: discipline ENABLES leverage (no anti-leverage moralizing). Pure-value, no CTA, no placeholders → ready.
body:
🧠 50-100x isn't the problem. Unsized 50-100x is.

The scene:
A setup lines up. You go in at 75x — but you "feel good," so you push the size way past your rule. No SL set yet, "I'll watch it." Price wicks the wrong way for ninety seconds and the position is gone. You blame the leverage.

The real mistake:
It was never the leverage. It was running big leverage with no stop and no fixed size. Leverage didn't blow the account — the missing SL and the oversized bet did.

The principle:
High leverage is a tool, not a sin. What separates an operator from a gambler is two boring habits:
• Strict sizing — your risk-per-trade is decided BEFORE entry and never gets "felt" up in the moment.
• SL before entry — every single time, placed where the idea is actually wrong (and off the liquidation magnet).

Do those two things and 50-100x becomes a precision instrument: small, controlled risk per trade, big asymmetry when you're right. Skip them and even 5x will bury you.

The reframe:
We run aggressive leverage on purpose — boldly, and with receipts. The discipline isn't using less leverage. It's sizing it right and stopping out fast when you're wrong. That's why we post our losses: every red trade you'll see from us had its SL do its job. Controlled loss, account intact, next setup.

50-100x done with a stop, not a prayer.

#MindOverMarket
